arts.ttl: Ipswich Town: Cameron Burgess set for new Australia manager
arts.oTtl: East Anglican Daily Times - Ispwich Town FC
arts.sum: As a result, Arnold has made the decision to resign ahead of the October international break, with Football Australia confirming that they will 'immediately commence the search for a new head coach as the Subway Socceroos'. “I would like to sincerely thank everyone, particularly the Australian football family, who has been part of this journey over the past six years and wish this incredible team and support staff nothing but success for this FIFA World Cup qualifying campaign."

arts.ttl: Ipswich Town: Sammie Szmodics discusses his career and summer transfer
arts.oTtl: East Anglican Daily Times - Ispwich Town FC
arts.sum: “If you told me when I was 18 years old, on loan at Braintree under the Cowley brothers, that in 10 years’ time you’d be in the Premier League and you’ll score at Manchester City, I would’ve laughed at you, but that’s the journey I’ve been on.” We just got relegated from the Championship and I told him that I still believe I’m good enough to play in the Premier League.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta's telling response when asked about Aaron Ramsdale after David Raya heroics
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta surprised many Arsenal fans this summer as he chose to sell Aaron Ramsdale and install David Raya as the Gunners’ number one but the decision appears to have been a masterstroke

arts.ttl: Ipswich Town: Kieran McKenna on renewing rivalry with Southampton
arts.oTtl: East Anglican Daily Times - Ispwich Town FC
arts.sum: Jeremy Sarmiento's dramatic 97th winner against Southampton at Portman Road is the most iconic moment from Ipswich's Championship promotion season. Omari Hutchinson scored in a 1-0 victory on the south coast last September, then Jeremy Sarmiento bagged a 97th goal to seal a dramatic 3-2 comeback win at Portman Road on Easter Monday.

arts.ttl: Preview: Southampton vs. Ipswich Town - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's Premier League clash between Southampton and Ipswich Town, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Southampton lineup vs. Ipswich: Predicted XI for Premier League clash
arts.sum: Sports Mole takes an in-depth look at how Southampton could line up for Saturday's Premier League clash with Ipswich Town.

arts.ttl: Ipswich lineup vs. Southampton: Predicted XI for Premier League clash

arts.ttl: Ipswich Town: Nathan Broadhead fit for Southampton trip
arts.oTtl: East Anglican Daily Times - Ispwich Town FC
arts.sum: Striker George Hirst and winger Wes Burns both returned from injuries in last weekend's 0-0 draw at Brighton, with right-back Harry Clarke, who underwent Achilles surgery in May, now the only player in the first team squad not fully fit. "I think from a physical point of view he would be able to play some minutes this weekend, which is a big positive," said McKenna, speaking at his pre-match press conference.

arts.ttl: Kieran McKenna does not see Ipswich’s trip to Southampton as a six-pointer
arts.sum: Ipswich and Southampton are yet to win in the Premier League this season, but McKenna’s team have taken points off Fulham and Brighton.
